name: QuantStats

agent_readiness.notes: Portfolio performance and risk analytics library for quantitative finance. Generate HTML tearsheet reports with one line. Metrics: Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, Max Drawdown, VaR, Expected Shortfall, Omega ratio. Monthly returns heatmaps, drawdown analysis, rolling statistics. Compare strategy to benchmark. Used by quant traders and portfolio managers for post-trade analysis.

gotchas: Slow development — library not actively maintained
Some metrics calculations have edge case bugs
Report generation requires specific pandas/matplotlib versions
No live portfolio tracking — analysis only
best_when: Generating portfolio performance tearsheets for backtesting results
Presenting strategy metrics (Sharpe, drawdown) to stakeholders
Post-trade performance analysis of algorithmic strategies
Comparing multiple strategy variants against benchmark
avoid_when: Production risk management systems (use dedicated risk platforms)
When pyfolio would serve same need (similar functionality)
summary: QuantStats is a Python library for portfolio performance analysis. Generates comprehensive HTML tearsheet reports with Sharpe, Sortino, Max Drawdown, VaR, and 50+ metrics in one line. Monthly returns heatmaps, drawdown analysis, benchmark comparison. Used by quant traders for presenting backtesting results. Limited maintenance — check for forks if encountering bugs.
